SICILY. Syracuse. Dionysius I (405-367 BC). AV 100-litrai or decadrachm (14mm, 5.78 gm, 2h). NGC MS★ 5/5 - 5/5, Fine Style. Attic standard. Ca. 400-370 BC. ΣΥΡΑΚΟΣΙΩΝ, head of Arethusa left i
sales.description:
Ancients
SICILY. Syracuse. Dionysius I (405-367 BC). AV 100-litrai or decadrachm (14mm, 5.78 gm, 2h). NGC MS★ 5/5 - 5/5, Fine Style. Attic standard. Ca. 400-370 BC. ΣΥΡΑΚΟΣΙΩΝ, head of Arethusa left in high classical style, hair pulled back into parallel waves along brow line, back held in sphendone decorated with two eight-pointed stars; pellet before, eight-pointed star behind head / Nude youthful Heracles kneeling to right, arms wrapped around neck of Nemean lion crouching left, his head pr...

sales.title:
Heritage World Coin Auctions - NYINC Signature Sale 3129, Lot 31001 — Ancients
SICILY. Syracuse. Dionysius I (405-370 BC). AR decadrachm (34mm, 43.12 gm, 2h). NGC Choice XF★ 5/5 - 5/5, Fine Style. Reverse die signed by Euainetos, ca. 400 BC. Charioteer driving quadriga
sales.description:
Ancients
SICILY. Syracuse. Dionysius I (405-370 BC). AR decadrachm (34mm, 43.12 gm, 2h). NGC Choice XF★ 5/5 - 5/5, Fine Style. Reverse die signed by Euainetos, ca. 400 BC. Charioteer driving quadriga galloping left, reins in left hand, kentron in right; Nike flying right in field above to crown him, military arms, including aspis (shield), greaves, cuirass, and crested Attic helmet, all joined by horizontal spear, ΑΘΛΑ ("prizes") below all in exergue, dotted border / ΣΥΡΑΚΟΣΙΩΝ, head of Arethu...

sales.title: Ira & Larry Goldberg Coins & Collectibles - Auction 150, Lot 436 — Sicily, Syracuse. Ca. 425-400 BC. AE Tetras (17 mm) 4.44 g. Head of Arethusa facing left. Reverse: Octopus; three pellets. NGC XF*, Strike: 5/5, Surface: 4/5. A magnificent piece with glossy Reddish-b
sales.description:
Sicily, Syracuse. Ca. 425-400 BC. AE Tetras (17 mm) 4.44 g. Head of Arethusa facing left. Reverse: Octopus; three pellets. NGC XF*, Strike: 5/5, Surface: 4/5. A magnificent piece with glossy Reddish-brown patina. NGC graded EF-40.  Estimated Value $250 - UP
Ex The Peter Preovolos Collection....

sales.title: Ira & Larry Goldberg Coins & Collectibles - Auction 150, Lot 438 — Syracuse. Electrum 50 Litrai (3.4g), 317-289 BC. Laureate hd. of Apollo l., symbol Kantharos behind. Reverse:  Tripod. HGC Vol. 2, 1294; Jenkins 1968. NGC Fine; Strike: 4/5, Surface: 5/5. Bold and wel
sales.description:
Syracuse. Electrum 50 Litrai (3.4g), 317-289 BC. Laureate hd. of Apollo l., symbol Kantharos behind. Reverse:  Tripod. HGC Vol. 2, 1294; Jenkins 1968. NGC Fine; Strike: 4/5, Surface: 5/5. Bold and well centered, even wear on both sides.  Estimated Value $400 - UP
Ex Peter Preovolos Collection....
